If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel!
___

**Version Number:** v9.3.32-0
**Reproducible in staging?:** Yes
**Reproducible in production?:** Yes
**If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/84285
**Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+bp0502fab@applause.expensifail.com
**Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team
**Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution
**Device used:** MacOS Monterey 12.7.4 / Chrome
**App Component:** Other

## Action Performed:
Precondition: workspace with added expensify card and travel feture configured.

1. Open any workspace's Expensify Card page
2. Open card settings 
3. Settlement frequency display correctly
4. Open Travel Invoicing page
5. Change Settlement frequency
6. Navigate back to Expensify Card page > open Settlement frequency
7. Change frequency

## Expected Result:
User is back to Settings tab with new frequency opened

## Actual Result:
Not here tab opens

## Workaround:
Unknown

## Platforms:
- [ ] Android: App
- [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome
- [ ] iOS: App
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome
- [x] Windows: Chrome
- [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.32-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-7762/folder/13176709/41236912/1232117774?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Windows 11/ Chrome **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Pre-requisite: User has a control workspace with 3 or more member in the workspace. Steps: 1. sign in to staging.new.expensify.com with an account that fulfils the pre-requisite above 2. Navigate to Workspace > Workspace settings > Workflows 3. Enable approvals (If not enabled) 4. Click on add approvals 5. Select 1 or 2 members in the "Expenses from" RHP and click Next 6. Add approver and finish the flow 7. Click on add approvals again ## Expected Result: Members from step 5 should not be displayed as a workflow already exists for the members. ## Actual Result: Members with an existing approvals flow are displayed in the "Expenses from" RHP when adding a new approval. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cb48a2a1-ac2b-4abd-af14-93a6e7bd1921 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @JmillsExpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1772736528071139) ## Action Performed: 1. A migrate customer attempts to cancel their account. 2. The user follows the Concierge message instructions to remove all workspace members first. 3. The user proceeds to remove workspace members. 4. While doing so, the system displays a full page of RBR (Request Blocked by Rules) errors. ## Expected Result: The system should display clear and actionable error messages explaining what requirement is blocking the action. ## Actual Result: The user receives multiple RBR errors on the page. (There was a problem removing that workspace member) Only one of the messages clearly explains the issue, while the others are unclear and not actionable. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The link for the fullstory is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1772736528071139) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Open the app and navigate to a page with an auto-focused input field (e.g., DM or Magic Code screen) 2. Quit the app 3. Reopen the app ## Expected Result: The splash screen should appear first, and the keyboard should only appear after the app finishes loading the screen. ## Actual Result: The keyboard appears on top of the splash screen before the app finishes loading. ## Notes: More noticeable on slower devices. Reproduced on iPhone 12 mini when multiple apps are open. Observed on: Magic code screen (Biometric test on Troubleshoot page) DM screen ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The video for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1772739317189249) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Receive a chat prompt requesting a receipt for an Expensify Card expense 2. Add the receipt to the expense 3. Wait several hours (or revisit the inbox later) 4. Open New Expensify Inbox 5. Observe the unread chat prompting the same action 6. Refresh the page 7. Check the expense in Classic and confirm the receipt is already attached ## Expected Result: The chat should update and no longer show as unread. ## Actual Result: The chat remains unread Refreshing the page does not update the state Classic confirms the receipt is already attached ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshot and Onyx data in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1772493155591779) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Report -“Changed approver” system message show in ND for reports created and autopaid in OD

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.30-2 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-7269/folder/13176702/41236801/1223430640?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Win 10 / Chrome **App Component:** Chat Report View ## Action Performed: Setup 1. Create a Control policy, on Instant submit, with Submit & Approve enabled 2. In OldDot - in the workspace Workflows tab, set Manually approve all expenses over: to $0.01 3. In ND, go to WS Workflows tab in "Make or track payments" section > Enable Auto-pay option to Reports above $100.00 are not reimbursed unless manually initiated. 4. Add a withdrawal-ready bank account for the workspace - Full verified BAV 5. In Wallet, create a Personal Bank Account - Use the Manual deposit bank account Steps 1. In OldDot - as the workspace owner, create a report & add an expense of $1, then approve the report 7. In NewDot, open the report 8. Verify you see the "paid with bank account xxx via workspace rules" system message copy. Result: Extra “Changed approver [owner name]” system message displayed in NewDot 9. In NewDot, Submit and approve another expense. 10. Verify that report also gets auto-reimbursed & shows the same system message copy as above. Result: "paid with bank account xxx via workspace rules" (expected) ## Expected Result: When a report is created and approved in OldDot, and then viewed in NewDot, only the system message “automatically paid $amount with Expensify via workspace rules” should be displayed. ## Actual Result: When the report is created and approved in OldDot, then opened in NewDot, an additional system message appears: “changed approver to [owner name]”, followed by the expected “automatically paid $amount with Expensify via workspace rules” message. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/909476c8-6ab7-405b-9695-94efd6a6a5f1 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @linhvovan29546
